target.title: test(playback): guard repeated barge-in audio_end_ms against regression
target.body:
## Summary

`BUGS-ANALYSIS.md` noted that a second `speech_started` event could send `audio_end_ms` larger than the item's actual audio length, causing an OpenAI API error. The referenced implementation used `lastAssistantItemId` / `lastAssistantItemStartedAtMs` variables that no longer exist — the playback tracker refactoring (merged in PR #5) already addressed the root cause:

- `clear()` resets both `pending` and `playedMsByItem` on every barge-in, so accumulated played-ms from a previous item cannot carry into the next one.
- `truncation()` caps `playedWithinChunkMs` at `chunk.durationMs`, so elapsed wall-clock time beyond the chunk's audio length is safely clamped.
- A stale Twilio mark arriving after `clear()` hits an empty `pending` list (index -1) and is ignored — it cannot corrupt `playedMsByItem`.

No code change is required. This PR adds two regression tests encoding the invariant.

## Real behavior proof

Ran the actual `vox serve` process with `OPENAI_REALTIME_URL` pointing at a local fake Realtime stand-in and a Twilio-protocol WebSocket caller sending continuous media events (advancing the media clock). The provider sent two audio bursts (itemX = 500ms, itemY = 300ms) with a barge-in after each one.

Provider log (redacted):
```
2026-06-19T18:01:46.323Z provider: -> response.output_audio.delta item=itemX (500ms audio)
2026-06-19T18:01:46.725Z provider: -> input_audio_buffer.speech_started (barge-in #1 on itemX)
2026-06-19T18:01:46.727Z provider: <- conversation.item.truncate {item_id:itemX, audio_end_ms:380}
2026-06-19T18:01:47.028Z provider: -> response.output_audio.delta item=itemY (300ms audio)
2026-06-19T18:01:47.429Z provider: -> input_audio_buffer.speech_started (barge-in #2 on itemY)
2026-06-19T18:01:47.430Z provider: <- conversation.item.truncate {item_id:itemY, audio_end_ms:300}

=== PROOF SUMMARY ===
  conversation.item.truncate events received: 2
  itemX (500ms total): truncated at 380ms — within bounds
  itemY (300ms total): truncated at 300ms — within bounds
  RESULT: PASS — audio_end_ms never exceeded item audio length
```

Both barge-ins produced bounded `audio_end_ms` values: itemX was truncated at 380ms (below its 500ms total), and itemY was truncated at 300ms (at its 300ms total — edge case where all audio was queued but none acked). Neither exceeds the item's audio length.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm run lint`
- [x] `pnpm run typecheck`
- [x] `pnpm test` — 58/58 passing, including the two new regression tests
- [x] `pnpm run build`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: joevenner
